hypothesis: If a molecular representation model is trained to align 3D electron density grids with SMILES strings via multimodal contrastive learning, then the resulting 3D embeddings will exhibit emergent pose-invariance to arbitrary molecular rotations without explicit equivariance constraints, because the SMILES modality encodes rotation-free chemical identity and the contrastive objective forces the 3D encoder to learn representations that capture chemically meaningful features rather than orientation-specific spatial information.
